cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. The question is asking why the employer made all those criticisms about Julia Vassilyevna's work. The options are A through D. First, I need to recall the story. The employer starts by saying they agreed on 30 rubles a month, but Julia had said 40. He then starts deducting various things: Sundays, holidays, days the kids were sick, her having a toothache, breaking a teacup, the kid climbing a tree and tearing his jacket, the maid stealing shoes, and some money he claims he gave her. He keeps subtracting amounts until he gives her 11 rubles. Julia doesn't protest and just says "merci." Then he gets angry because she's not protesting and realizes he's cheated her. He ends up giving her 80 rubles and apologizes, thinking about how easy it is to crush the weak. So, the employer's criticisms seem to be part of a strategy to reduce her pay.
